plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure for capital preservation.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to de-risk and align with conservative allocation.
Initiate a small position in ZS at 5% as a top-quality name from the scanner.
Hold a significant cash buffer to enhance capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk. Expected drawdowns should be lower due to de-risking.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure and exit flagged positions.
Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to concentration limits and manage broad index exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to manage overall market exposure and free up capital.
Trim SMH to 8% as it is flagged by the scanner and in a downtrend.
Initiate a position in ZS at 8% as a strong, high-quality tech setup.
Add ASML at 7% from the scanner's buy list, focusing on quality tech growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. Market volatility will still impact returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to 10% each, shifting focus from income/broad index to aggressive growth.
Re-establish a small SOXL position at 5% to lean into leveraged semiconductors, with strict stop discipline.
Initiate a substantial position in ZS at 12% as the highest conviction scanner setup.
Add NVDA at 10% and ASML at 8% from the scanner's buy list, leveraging strong tech setups.
Maintain a small cash buffer and adhere to strict stops for all positions.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth by leaning into leverage and high-conviction setups. This approach significantly amplifies both potential upside and drawdown, with leveraged ETFs subject to decay and rebalancing. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher risk.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta SMH to reduce risk to near zero.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce exposure to single names and high-beta tech.
Reduce SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital.
Sell QQQ to shift away from growth-oriented broad index exposure.
Increase QQQI significantly to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer to further stabilize the portfolio for income generation.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield and monthly distributions. It significantly cuts leverage and high-beta names, prioritizing low drawdown. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so this is not a savings account.
